rulings.Case Title: FUND RECOVERY SERVICES, INC. VS. EDWARD WARREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 16, 2013, Line 1. Plaintiff FUND RECOVERY SERVICES, INC.'s Motion to Compel Answers to Post-Judgment Discovery and for Award of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Barri Bonapart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: There is no opposition to the motion. It is ordered that the motion is Granted. Defendant is to provide verified responses without objections to Plaintiff's Post Judgment Interrogatories and Demand for Production of Documents as authorized by CCP § 708.020 and § 708.030 on or before June 6, 2013. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant shall pay a monetary sanction to Plaintiff in the sum of $300 on or before June 6, 2013 for failure to respond and/or to meet and confer regarding said failure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Barri@treelaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARILOU D SAMSON VS. RUTH CUSING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT RUTH CUSING's Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Prosecution. Granted. Plaintiff did not bring this action to trial within 5 years of filing the action on June 6, 2007. (CCP § 583.310.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the 5 year period should be tolled on any of the grounds enumerated in CCP § 583.340. Therefore, dismissal is mandatory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA VILLANUEVA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUARDIAN AD et al VS. SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT JAY TRISKO's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains Defendant's demurrer in part and overrules in part. Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's breach of contract cause of action for failure to state a claim is sustained without leave to amend. The delayed discovery rule can be applied to contract claims in limited cases. (See e.g. April Enterprises, Inc. v. KTTV (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 805, 828; Weatherly v. Universal Music Pub. Grp. (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 913, 920.) While Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint alleges that Plaintiff was ignorant of Defednant Trisko's misrepresentations until documents were turned over in September 2012, Plaintiff was still aware of the rescission of the workers' compensation policy. Consequently, when Plaintiff filed the second Amended Complaint on October 22, 2012, adding Defendant Trisko, it was not within the 2 year statute of limitations under CCP § 339(1). Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's negligence cause of action for failure to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that the accrual of the cause of action was postponed because Plaintiff could not have discovered Trisko's alleged failure to obtain the requested worker's compensation insurance for Plaintiff A&F until documents were made available to Villanueava's counsel in September, 2012. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797. 807.) Defendants demurrer Plaintiff's negligence cause of action for uncertainty is sustained with leave to amend. Villanueva alleges that A&F has assigned all its claims against D McGovern, and its agents and representatives for any negligence claims (See TAC 16.) Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to put Defendant Trisko on notice as to what issues must be admitted or denied because the Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint is unclear as to which of the named Plaintiffs is claiming negligence against Trisko. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Plaintiff is given 10 court days to amend if they can, in good faith, allege facts as to which Plaintiff is bringing a claim of negligence against Defendant Trisko. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HUSTON JACKSON VS. GETHSEMANE BAPTIST CHURCH A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Responses To Req. To Produce Documents, Sets One And Two; To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Req. For Sanctions Against All Deft'S Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 16, 2013, Line 3. Plaintiff HUSTON JACKSON'S Motion to Compel Responses to Request to Produce Documents, Sets One and Two; to Compel Responses to Interrogatories and Request for Sanctions Against All Defendant Jointly and Severally Pro Tem Judge Barri Bonapart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: There is no opposition to the motion. It is ordered that the Motion is Granted. Each Defendant is to provide verified answers without objections to Plaintiff's Specially Prepared Interrogatories and Request for Production of Documents, Sets One and Two, on or before May 24, 2013. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that defendants shall pay monetary sanction to Jackson's attorney in the sum of $1,500 on or before May 28, 2013. Monetary sanctions against defendants for its neglect of discovery obligations, notwithstanding the many meet and confer efforts to elicit defendant's cooperation. Each defendant is ordered to pay its pro rata share of that amount. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Plaintiff's request for evidentiary sanctions is hereby denied as this is the first effort Plaintiff has made to compel responses to discovery. The issue of evidentiary sanctions may be raised at the time of trial should defendants fail to comply with this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Barri@treelaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS SALAS VS. BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. and BAE SYSTEMS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. Bae Systems, Inc. is a proper defendant as Mr. Salas alleges that he was employed by both SFSR and Bae Systems, Inc. Further, Mr. Salas has pled sufficient facts to allege cause of action based on FEHA violations. Defendants raise questions of fact not subject to a demurrer.
